properties.name: waiver:status
properties.value: EMPTY — no active waivers. The Python 3.10+ migration (py310-migration) cleared the entire RR-4 cluster; pip-audit passes with 0 findings and NO --ignore-vuln flags in the Makefile or .github/workflows/ci.yml.

properties.name: waiver:resolved_rr4
properties.value: The 19 RR-4 advisories (requests, urllib3, streamlit, pillow ×6, pyarrow, msgpack, filelock ×2, pytest, pip ×3) each had a published fix whose first fixed release required Python >=3.10. With requires-python now >=3.10, a clean resolution installs the fixed versions (requests>=2.33, urllib3>=2.7, streamlit>=1.54, pillow>=12.2, pyarrow>=23, msgpack>=1.2.1, filelock>=3.20.3, pytest>=9.0.3, pip>=26.1.2), pinned reproducibly in uv.lock. All 19 IDs removed from the audit waiver.

properties.name: waiver:resolved_rr1
properties.value: GHSA-4xh5-x5gv-qwph / CVE-2025-8869 (pip fallback tar extraction) is cleared by pip>=25.3 (PEP 706 data filter) on the >=3.10 floor; the installed pip (>=26.1.2) is no longer reported by pip-audit. RR-1 un-ignored per its own ‘un-ignore once a fix ships’ review policy.

properties.name: waiver:review_cadence
properties.value: Quarterly. Re-introduce a justified entry (byte-identical in Makefile + ci.yml + here) only if a genuinely-unfixable advisory appears.
